goal: Four-line cheat sheet mapping prim/xform/articulation/joint to Unity concepts where they exist.
thoughts: Ground definitions in real Isaac Sim 5.x terminology via lookup_knowledge. Honest mappings: prim ~ any scene-tree node, xform ~ Unity Transform, joint ~ joint/fixed-joint, articulation = no real Unity equivalent (the whole robot as one physics object). Follow with a click-and-see walkthrough of the loaded robot's tree.
code:
kb = lookup_knowledge(
    query="Isaac Sim 5.x glossary prim xform articulation joint Unity equivalents"
)
tree = list_all_prims(path_prefix="/World/Robot", max_depth=4)
# Walk the scene tree:
# - click a joint prim -> see body0/body1 relationships
# - click the articulation root -> see it owns all the joints
starters = scene_aware_starter_prompts(
    user_goal="understand scene tree concepts",
    user_level="beginner"
)
failure_modes: Pasting the USD schema hierarchy PDF
Claiming articulation maps cleanly to Unity prefab (it does not)
Lecturing on composition arcs before he has clicked a prim
